subject_profile.summary: Base country-level assessment; default category weights prioritize rule of law, institutional integrity, and personal freedom.
stated_values: Rule of law and institutional integrity weighted most heavily
Personal freedom and security from arbitrary state action are foundational
Economic success is contingent on rule of law, not a substitute for it

flags: Generated 2026-05-28 under v2.0: multilateral + independent (IMF, World Bank, ADB, Freedom House, RSF, TI, Lowy, WHO) + international press; state media EXCLUDED on governance.

The Pacific's strongest peaceful-transfer track record under stress - it survived the tense 2021 HRPP-ending transfer and a 2025 mid-term crisis (a FAST split, a budget defeat, an Aug 2025 snap election) entirely through legal/electoral channels.

Standout features: the best regional macro story (debt ~27.7% of GDP, a moderate distress rating, inflation near target) and strong social cohesion, against a real press-freedom slide, a crushing NCD burden, and cyclone/tsunami exposure.

economic.notes: The best regional balance sheet (low debt, near-target inflation), on a tiny remittance-dependent base.

fiscal_state.notes: Public debt ~27.7% of GDP (a 20-year low), upgraded high-to-moderate distress - strong but structurally aid/remittance-dependent. (IMF Art IV 2025)

monetary_independence.notes: The Central Bank of Samoa manages the tala against a basket - limited autonomy, modest toolkit. (IMF; CBS)

reserve_currency_intl_monetary.notes: The tala is not a reserve currency; FX reserves are comfortably above the optimal level. (definitional; IMF 2025)

inflation.notes: Down from double digits (FY23) to ~2.9% y/y, stabilizing near the 3% target. (IMF 2025)

banking_stability.notes: A small, shallow system - adequate but with thin supervisory data. (IMF; ADB)

trade_actions_capacity.notes: A narrow export base (fish, agriculture, tourism), import-dependent, with ~30%-of-GDP remittances; open and undiversified. (IMF; World Bank)

institutional.notes: Credible courts and comparatively low corruption, against a clear press-freedom deterioration.

rule_of_law.notes: Courts adjudicated the 2021 crisis and 2025 disputes credibly; corruption is comparatively low, with periodic executive-judiciary friction. (Freedom House 2025; TI)

statistical_integrity.notes: A functioning Bureau of Statistics with donor support and standard small-state capacity limits. (IMF; SBS)

civil_service_capacity.notes: Reasonable for its size with relatively strong service delivery, but small and stretched. (World Bank; IMF)

press_freedom.notes: RSF rank slid 22-to-44 (2024-25) on 'authoritarian pressure' and online death threats against journalists - a clear deterioration. (RSF 2025)

political_social.notes: A turbulent-but-durably-democratic, highly cohesive matai society.

political_stability.notes: A genuine 2025 crisis (FAST split, budget defeat, a snap poll) resolved peacefully and constitutionally - turbulent but durably democratic. (Wikipedia 2025 election; RNZ)

civil_liberties.notes: Broadly respected freedoms, with the matai-only candidacy rule and recent journalist harassment as caveats. (Freedom House 2025)

treatment_non_citizens.notes: Not a major migration destination, with no notable mistreatment record. (inference)

social_cohesion.notes: A cohesive, ethnically homogeneous (~96% Samoan) society organized around communal matai governance, with conflicts settled non-violently. (CIVICUS; World Bank)

demographics.notes: Slow growth with persistent net out-migration to NZ/Australia/US - remittance-positive but a long-run workforce drag. (World Bank; SBS)

geopolitical.notes: Strong NZ/Australia ties with China engagement, non-aligned and conflict-free.

alliance_reliability.notes: Strong NZ/Australia ties plus engagement with China; hosted CHOGM 2024 - non-aligned and pragmatic. (Lowy 2025)

conflict_involvement.notes: No military conflict; remote from any war theatre. (inference)

sanctions_capital_controls.notes: An open, unsanctioned economy; legacy Chinese project debt is a fiscal not a sanctions issue. (IMF; Lowy 2025)

physical_practical.notes: Relatively better regional infrastructure, against a crushing NCD burden and cyclone/tsunami exposure.

climate.notes: Cyclone/SLR/coastal-erosion exposure (and the 2009 tsunami) concentrated on the low coastal strip where most live. (WB Pacific CCDR)

healthcare.notes: A severe NCD burden (NCDs ~83% of 2021 deaths, ~58% female obesity) and the 2019 measles legacy, with improving World-Bank-supported primary care. (WHO; World Bank 2025)

infrastructure.notes: Relatively better than regional peers, boosted by CHOGM-related investment, but small and disaster-vulnerable. (IMF 2025; World Bank)

crime_safety.notes: Generally low violent crime, with petty crime present. (inference; Freedom House 2025)

disaster_insurance.notes: Negligible catastrophe-insurance penetration against real cyclone/tsunami exposure. (inference)

summary: The region's resilience story: it absorbed both the tense 2021 transfer that ended decades of HRPP rule and a sharp 2025 mid-term crisis - a FAST party split, a budget voted down, and an August snap election that installed La'auli Schmidt as PM - entirely through courts and ballots, without violence. Macro fundamentals are the strongest in this group: public debt at a two-decade low (~27.7% of GDP), an upgraded debt-distress rating, inflation back near target, and ample reserves, though the economy stays tiny, remittance-dependent, and undiversified. The clear deteriorations are a steep press-freedom slide (RSF 22-to-44 in one year, amid threats against journalists) and a crushing NCD/obesity health burden. The skew is roughly symmetric with a modest negative disaster tail.
